Results.Name: havenversion
Results.Label: Self test: HCC version is latest major or within 3 months upgrade window
Results.Rationale: Haven clusters must stay up to date

Results.Name: clusteradmin
Results.Label: Self test: does HCC have cluster-admin
Results.Rationale: In order for the Haven Compliancy Checker to function properly elevated privileges are required.

Results.Name: multiaz
Results.Label: Multiple availability zones in use
Results.Rationale: Running a cluster on a single availability zone means a higher risk of downtime when that single zone runs into problems.

Results.Name: hamasters
Results.Label: Running at least 3 master nodes
Results.Rationale: This ensures a highly available control plane.

Results.Name: haworkers
Results.Label: Running at least 3 worker nodes
Results.Rationale: This enables running highly available workloads.

Results.Name: nodehardening
Results.Label: Nodes have SELinux, Grsecurity, AppArmor, LKRG, Talos or Flatcar enabled
Results.Rationale: Security matters on every layer of a system and should an attacker break out of a deployment onto a node increased node security will help prevent further escalation.

Results.Name: privatenetworking
Results.Label: Private networking topology
Results.Rationale: Not directly exposing masters or workers to the public internet can increase the security of the cluster.

Results.Name: kubernetesversion
Results.Label: Kubernetes version is latest stable or max 3 minor versions behind
Results.Rationale: This allows cluster users to access new functionality quickly and encourages a well-implemented update mechanism.

Results.Name: rbac
Results.Label: Role Based Access Control is enabled
Results.Rationale: Basic security option which is enabled by default in order to control who can do what on a cluster.

Results.Name: rwxvolumes
Results.Label: ReadWriteMany persistent volumes support
Results.Rationale: This ensures that storage can be created which can be used by highly available deployments.

Results.Name: cncf
Results.Label: CNCF Kubernetes Conformance
Results.Rationale: Cloud Native Computing Foundation's Kubernetes checks ensure the Kubernetes cluster adheres to the standard Kubernetes API's.

Results.Name: autocerts
Results.Label: Automated HTTPS certificate provisioning
Results.Rationale: This makes it easy for engineers to expose an Ingress with a valid SSL certificate which automatically renews.

Results.Name: logs
Results.Label: Log aggregation is running
Results.Rationale: In order to be in control of the workload on a cluster it's mandatory to aggregate all container logs.

Results.Name: metrics
Results.Label: Metrics-server is running
Results.Rationale: In order to be in control of a cluster it's mandatory to have eyes and ears on the cluster resources.

Results.Name: shavalidation
Results.Label: SHA has been validated
Results.Rationale: Haven CLI should be validated by SHA

Results.Name: cis
Results.Label: CIS Kubernetes Security Benchmark
Results.Rationale: Center for Internet Security's Kubernetes benchmark assists in following the best practices of securing a cluster.

Results.Name: kubescape
Results.Label: Kubescape
Results.Rationale: Aqua Security's Kubescape tool assists in following the best practices of securing a cluster.
